draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer interfaces that feel inviting and human-centered rather than cold and corporate
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: The CRM should use soft lighting, warm color palettes, and approachable typography to feel less like enterprise software and more like a trusted colleague
facade.rejectImplies: Users may prefer crisp, cool-toned interfaces with high contrast and minimal ornamentation for clarity and efficiency

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer interfaces that feel inviting and human-centered rather than cold and corporate
evidence.implication: The CRM should use soft lighting, warm color palettes, and approachable typography to feel less like enterprise software and more like a trusted colleague

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a conversational, human tone over corporate formality in their CRM tool
facade.label: Casual and chatty
facade.acceptImplies: User values approachable, warm communication; tool should use contractions, humor, and personal touches; avoid stiff corporate language
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers polished, professional tone; CRM should feel authoritative and formal; minimize casual language and personality

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond to inviting, soft-lit interfaces that feel supportive rather than corporate
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward interfaces with warm color temperatures, soft shadows, and ambient lighting—suggests comfort-focused design direction
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clinical brightness, high contrast, or cool tones—suggests efficiency-focused, minimal-distraction aesthetic

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer immediate notification of client activity (messages, feedback, project changes) over batched or delayed notifications
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and Behavior — real-time responsiveness vs batched updates; push notifications (animated) vs periodic digests (static)
facade.label: Instant client updates
facade.acceptImplies: User values snappy, always-on communication flow; likely wants push notifications, live activity feeds, and immediate alerts in the CRM interface
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers calm, batch-based updates (daily digests, weekly summaries); likely wants to avoid notification fatigue and focus time; may favor scheduled check-ins over constant motion

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer visual scanning of client info (cards with key metrics) over dense tabular layouts
facade.label: Client list as cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick visual pattern-matching and status-at-a-glance over exhaustive data tables; likely wants dashboard-style summary cards for active clients, revenue, project status
facade.rejectImplies: User needs comprehensive, sortable, filterable data access; prefers tables or detailed views; skeptical of information hierarchy that hides fields; may want to bulk-edit or perform data-heavy operations

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
Whether user wants all tools visible at once vs. hidden behind menus/modals—reveals preference for dashboard density and navigation style in a tool-heavy CRM
Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business-like assistant or a supportive collaborator voice
Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals desired emotional tone while managing client work
Whether user prefers data-dense tables/lists vs. card-based summaries with key info highlighted—reveals how they want to scan and process client/project information
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. smooth, animated transitions—reveals preference for snappy responsiveness vs. polished, deliberate interactions in a fast-paced CRM

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants all tools visible at once vs. hidden behind menus/modals—reveals preference for dashboard density and navigation style in a tool-heavy CRM
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business-like assistant or a supportive collaborator voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals desired emotional tone while managing client work
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-dense tables/lists vs. card-based summaries with key info highlighted—reveals how they want to scan and process client/project information
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. smooth, animated transitions—reveals preference for snappy responsiveness vs. polished, deliberate interactions in a fast-paced CRM
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need interfaces that reduce cognitive load and decision fatigue, using breathing room and progressive disclosure rather than overwhelming density
facade.label: Calm dashboard
facade.acceptImplies: The CRM should prioritize spacious layouts, minimal visual hierarchy, and delayed information reveal to help freelancers feel less scattered when managing multiple clients and projects
facade.rejectImplies: Freelancers may prefer efficient, information-dense interfaces that show everything at once for quick scanning, or they may want more energetic, action-oriented visual feedback to stay motivated

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need a persistent navigation + scannable project view to manage multiple client relationships without losing context
facade.axisTargeted: Layout structure: persistent sidebar nav vs. tab-based navigation
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values persistent navigation and card-based layouts for CRM workflows
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer tab-based, full-width, or list-heavy interface structures instead
